card.intuition: Components that catch JavaScript errors anywhere in their child component tree, log those errors, and display a fallback UI instead of the component tree that crashed.
card.analogy: A fuse box: if a toaster in your kitchen (child component) short-circuits, the fuse 'trips' (Error Boundary). Your whole house doesn't burn down (the app doesn't white-screen); you just can't use the kitchen until you fix the toaster.
when_to_use: Preventing a single broken component from crashing the entire app
Showing 'Something went wrong, please try again' messages gracefully
gotchas: Error boundaries do NOT catch errors in: Event handlers, Asynchronous code (like fetch), or Server-side rendering
Currently, Error Boundaries MUST be Class Components in React (there is no functional hook equivalent yet)

steps.annotation: Normal render: component tree renders without errors.
A child component throws during render or lifecycle.
Error bubbles up to the nearest error boundary (class component).
getDerivedStateFromError sets hasError=true. Fallback UI renders instead of crashed subtree.
User clicks retry → boundary resets state → subtree re-mounts cleanly.

exercise.prompt: Identify the two specific lifecycle methods used to create an Error Boundary in a Class Component.
hints: One gets state from error, the other is 'did catch'
exercise.test_code:
assert "getDerived" in solution
assert "DidCatch" in solution
print("PASS")
